growth_records.cite: Akberdin, Ilya R. et al. Methane utilization in Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20ZR: a systems approach. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-018-20574-z. [PMC5802761]
growth_records.method: reported (specific growth rate from continuous culture, Table 1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.12=5.78 h
growth_records.snippet: the methane consumption rate was set at 11.7 ± 0.14 mmol g−1 DCW h−1 ... during continuous culture growth (at observed μ = 0.12 ± 0.01 h−1) ... oxygen consumption rate during exponential growth in continuous culture was 14.7 ± 0.9 mmol g−1
growth_records.notes: Continuous-culture (chemostat) condition, Table 1. Measured: qCH4=11.7±0.14, qO2=14.7±0.9 mmol/gDCW/h, O2/CH4=1.25±0.06, qCO2=0.37±0.05 mmol/gDCW/h, μ=0.12±0.01 h-1. td=ln2/0.12=5.775 h. Non-growth maintenance methane requirement measured separately = 3.6±0.3 mmol CH4/gDCW/h (maintenance only, not growth). Temperature/pH not explicitly stated in text (medium is alkaline carbonate-buffered, organism is haloalkaliphilic ~30C/pH9 by convention but NOT reported here, left null). Formate excretion reduced by tungsten supplementation (all reported experiments W-supplemented).

up.method: off-gas decline over time, batch culture, Table 1
off-gas decline over time, batch culture, Table 1

growth_records.cite: Akberdin, Ilya R. et al. Methane utilization in Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20ZR: a systems approach. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-018-20574-z. [PMC5802761]
growth_records.method: reported (specific growth rate, Table 1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.14=4.95 h
growth_records.snippet: Batch culture (10% CH4:Air) Methane Uptake (mmol g CDW−1 h−1) 18.6 ± 1.4 ... Oxygen Uptake 24.4 ± 2.1 ... O2:CH4 Uptake Ratio 1.31 ± 0.07 ... Specific Growth Rate (h−1) 0.14 ± 0.02
growth_records.notes: Batch condition, Table 1. Measured: qCH4=18.6±1.4, qO2=24.4±2.1 mmol/gDCW/h, O2:CH4=1.31±0.07, μ=0.14±0.02 h-1. td=ln2/0.14=4.951 h. Higher uptake rates than chemostat (higher gas partial pressures 10% CH4 vs 5%). Temperature/pH not stated in text (left null).

growth_records.cite: Nguyen LT et al. Biological conversion of methane to putrescine using genome-scale model-guided metabolic engineering of a methanotrophic bacterium Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20Z. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1490-z. [PMC6570963]
growth_records.snippet: The growth rate of the evolved strain was comparable to that of wild-type strain (0.089 h−1)
growth_records.notes: Wild-type M. alcaliphilum 20Z specific growth rate reported directly as 0.089 h−1. doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.089 = 7.79 h (calculated). Carbon source taken as 0.2% (v/v) methanol because the growth/ALE experiments (Fig.3, ALE section) were run in NMS + 0.2% methanol; the medium base NMS is the same used with methane elsewhere. pH not given numerically (haloalkaliphilic strain, medium buffered with bicarbonate/carbonate, alkaline). No measured methane/methanol uptake rate reported; only in-silico FBA/OptGene predictions exist (max theoretical putrescine yield 0.579 g/g-CH4, productivity 0.937 mmol/gDCW/h) which are model predictions, not experimental, so excluded from uptake/secretion.

growth_records.cite: Nguyen LT et al. Biological conversion of methane to putrescine using genome-scale model-guided metabolic engineering of a methanotrophic bacterium Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20Z. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1490-z. [PMC6570963]
growth_records.snippet: the evolved strain showed even slightly better growth rate in the presence of 100 and 200 mM putrescine (0.121 h−1 and 0.111 h−1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Evolved 20ZE strain grown in NMS (0.2% methanol) with 100 mM putrescine dihydrochloride stress; mu = 0.121 h−1 reported. doubling_time_h = ln2/0.121 = 5.73 h (calculated). Stress-condition record distinct by added putrescine concentration.

growth_records.cite: Nguyen LT et al. Biological conversion of methane to putrescine using genome-scale model-guided metabolic engineering of a methanotrophic bacterium Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20Z. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1490-z. [PMC6570963]
growth_records.snippet: the evolved strain showed even slightly better growth rate in the presence of 100 and 200 mM putrescine (0.121 h−1 and 0.111 h−1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Evolved 20ZE strain grown in NMS (0.2% methanol) with 200 mM putrescine dihydrochloride stress; mu = 0.111 h−1 reported. doubling_time_h = ln2/0.111 = 6.24 h (calculated).

growth_records.medium: NMS (nitrate mineral salt medium), methane as carbon source

sec.units: mg/L (titer, not a rate) after 268 h

growth_records.cite: Nguyen LT et al. Biological conversion of methane to putrescine using genome-scale model-guided metabolic engineering of a methanotrophic bacterium Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20Z. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1490-z. [PMC6570963]
growth_records.snippet: the experimental specific growth rate of the engineered strain (0.048 h−1) in NMS ... After 268 h of cultivation, the engineered strain 20ZE4A-pACO produced 39.14 ± 1.35 mg/L of putrescine in an NMS medium
growth_records.notes: Engineered 20ZE4A-pACO on methane in NMS: experimental mu = 0.048 h−1 (reported, higher than OptGene-predicted 0.032 h−1). doubling_time_h = ln2/0.048 = 14.44 h (calculated). Putrescine 39.14±1.35 mg/L is an end-point titer (concentration), not a specific rate; captured in secretion_rates with explicit unit note. No measured methane uptake rate given.

growth_records.medium: ANMS (ammonia nitrate mineral salt medium) = NMS + 2 mM NH4Cl, methane as carbon source

sec.units: mg/L (titer, not a rate) after 268 h

growth_records.cite: Nguyen LT et al. Biological conversion of methane to putrescine using genome-scale model-guided metabolic engineering of a methanotrophic bacterium Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20Z. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1490-z. [PMC6570963]
growth_records.snippet: In ANMS with 2 mM of ammonium chloride, the highest titer of 98.08 mg/L putrescine with a productivity of 2.9 nmol/gDCW/h and a yield of 0.0276 g-(putrescine)/g-CH4 was obtained.
growth_records.notes: Engineered 20ZE4A-pACO on methane in ANMS (NMS + 2 mM NH4Cl): highest experimental putrescine titer 98.08±2.86 mg/L, reported specific productivity 2.9 nmol/gDCW/h, experimental yield 0.0276 g-putrescine/g-CH4. Strain reached OD600 4.0 vs 2.5 for WT. Growth rate for this specific ANMS condition not stated numerically ('higher growth rate' qualitatively). No 5 mM NH4Cl growth (inhibited); 1 mM NH4Cl gave 59.46 mg/L (no rate). Compare OptGene-predicted (model, not experimental) yield 0.408 g/g-CH4 and productivity 0.66 mmol/gDCW/h for ACKr knockout — excluded from experimental fields.
